Fern Porrrrcer PRACrrcEs CouprrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886

Steven G. Churchwell
DLA Piper US, LLP
400 Capitol Mall, Suite 2400
Sacramento, CA 95814-4428
Re: Your Request for fnformal Assistance
Our File No.I-06-181
DearMr. Churchwell:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Mayor Steve Messina,
regarding the "the public generally''exception to the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act').1 Because your request seeks general 6;ridurr"", we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.2 Nothing in this letter should be
construed to evaluate any conduct that has already taken place.
We note that your original request for advice was made on behalf of Mayor
Messina as well as council members ElizabethPatterson, Alan Schwartzman,and Bi1
Whitney. However, for the purposes of this leffer, we are limiting our advice to Mayor
Messina's particular circumstances. As we discussed in a telephone conversation on
January 25, 2007, the Commission does not act as a finder of fact when providing advice,
and, ultimately, final factaal determinations must be left up to the public offi cials. (In re
Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Accordingly, we do noihave sufficient facts to reach
conclusions pertaining to Councilmember Patterson's, Councilmember Schwartzman,s,
or Councilmember Whitney's particular circumstances. Furthermore, we note that you
have informed us, in a telephone conversation on January 31,2007, that you are no
longer seeking advice pertaining to Councilmember Whitney.
QUESTION
Providing that Mayor Messina is disqualified under the Act's conflict of interest
provisions, does the "public generally" exception allow him to, nevertheless, make,
I Government Code sections 8 I 000-91 014. Commission regulations appear at title 2,sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 831 14; regulation 18329(c)(3).)

participate in making, or influence govefllment decisions regarding the adoption of a

CONCLUSION
No, the pubric generafly exception does not appear to appryto Mayor Messina,s
circumstances.

You have provided the following facts relevant to the analysis of the ..public

Mayot Messina owns two properties inside or within 500 feet of the Downtown
District including avacantlot and a rental propefty. Mayor Messina also owns an ice
cteam/sandwich shop in the Downtown District. As an idditionalnote, tvtayor Messina,s
economic interests are more thoroughly detailed in our previous letter issued to yorr,
C hurc hwe ll Advice Letter, No. 4-06- 1 3 0.
ANALYSIS
The "Public Generally, Exception
Even if a public official determines that his or her economic interests will
experience amateial financial effect as a result of the governmental decision before the
official, he or she aay still participateunder the "publii generally,, exception if the
mateial financial effect of a governmental decision o., alublic offi"iut,, economic
interests is indistinguishable from its effect on the public-generally. (Sectio n g7103;
regulation 18707.)
Regulation 18707 (b) sets forth a four-step process to determine,.if the effect of a
decision is not distinguishable from the effect oojn" public generally:,,
"(1) step one: Identify each specific person or real property (economic
interest) that is materially affected bythe gorr"*-"ituia""irior.
"(2) step Two: For each person or real property identified in Step onq
determine the_applicable 'significant segment, rule according to ihe
provisions of [regulation 1 8707. I (b)].
"(3) step Three: Determine if the- significant segment is affected by the
governmental decision as set forth in the applicable 'significant s"fuent,
rule. If the answer is 'no,' then the analysis ends because the first[rong
of a two-part test set forth in [regulati on lg707.l (b)] is not met, ua ilrJ
public official cannot participate in the gove*menial decision. If the
answer is 'yes,' proceed to Step Four.
"(4) Step Four: Followirig the provisions of [regulation lg707.l(b)e)],
determine if the person or real property identitea in Step one is affected
by the governmental decision in 'substantially the same manner, as other
persons or real property in the applicable significant segment. If the
answer is 'yes' as to each person or real property identified in Step one,
then the effect of the decision is not distinguishubl. fro* the effect on the
public genetaTly and the public official miy particrpate in the decision. If
the answer is 'no' as to any person or real propertyidentified in Step one,
the public official may not participate in the glvernmental decision unless
one of the special rules set forth in [reguration s 1g707.2 through rg707 ..g]

(Regulation 1 87 07 (b)(t) - (4).)

Downtown District will affect all residents living downtown, the minimal threshold of ten
